channel.title: IT Arch
channel.description: A blog about software engineering, technology and ideas.

item.title: The __set_name__ method for descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
DeôÙscripôÙtors genôÙerôÙalôÙly have to inôÙterôÙact with atôÙtributes of the manôÙaged obôÙjecôÙt, and this is done by inôÙspectôÙing __ôÙdicôÙt__ on that obôÙject (or callôÙing getatôÙtr/seôÙtatôÙtr, but the probôÙlem is the same), and findôÙing the key unôÙderôÙ the speôÙcifôÙic name.

For this reaôÙson, the deôÙscripôÙtor will have to know the name of the key to look ôÙfor, which is reôÙlatôÙed to the name of the atôÙtribute is manôÙagôÙing.

On preôÙviôÙous verôÙsions of Python this had to be done exôÙplicôÙitôÙly. If we wantôÙed to ôÙwork around it, there were some more adôÙvanced ways to do so. LuckôÙiôÙly, afôÙter PEP-487 (added in Python 3.6), there are some enôÙhanceôÙments reôÙgardôÙing class creôÙation, which alôÙso afôÙfectôÙs deôÙscripôÙtors.

Let's reôÙview the probôÙlem, the preôÙviôÙous apôÙproachôÙes to tackôÙle it, and the modôÙern way of solvôÙing it.

Read moreãÎ (4 min remaining to read)

item.title: Descriptors & Decorators
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
DeôÙscripôÙtors are an amazôÙing tool to have in our toolôÙbox, as they come in handy in many opôÙporôÙtuôÙniôÙties.

ProbôÙaôÙbly the best thing about deôÙscripôÙtors, is that they can imôÙprove othôÙer ôÙsoôÙluôÙtionôÙs. Let's see how we can write betôÙter decôÙoôÙraôÙtors, by usôÙing deôÙscripôÙtors.

Read moreãÎ (5 min remaining to read)

item.title: Types of Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
ReôÙsumôÙing from where we left ofôÙf, on the preôÙviôÙous post, on which we took A first look at deôÙscripôÙtors, it's time to exôÙplore their difôÙferôÙent typeôÙs and how they work inôÙterôÙnalôÙly.

In Python, almost everything is represented with a dictionary. Objects are dictionaries. Classes are objects, hence they also are contained into a dictionary. This is denoted by the __dict__ attribute that objects have.

There are two types of descriptors: data descriptors and non-data ones. If a descriptor implements both [1] __get__() and __set__(), it's called a data descriptor; otherwise is a non-data descriptor.

Read moreãÎ (7 min remaining to read)

item.title: A first look at descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
DeôÙscripôÙtors are one of the most powôÙerôÙful feaôÙtures of Python. The reaôÙson why they're so powôÙerôÙful is beôÙcause they enôÙable us to conôÙtrol the core opôÙerôÙaôÙtions (get, set, delete) [1], of an atôÙtribute in a givôÙen obôÙjecôÙt, so that we can hook a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar code, conôÙtrolled by us, in orôÙder to modôÙiôÙfy, change, or exôÙtend the oôÙrigôÙiôÙnal opôÙerôÙaôÙtion.

Read moreãÎ (8 min remaining to read)

item.title: Upcoming talk at PyCon CZ 2017
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
The proôÙposôÙal of my talk tiôÙtled "DisôÙcovôÙerôÙing DeôÙscripôÙtors", was acôÙceptôÙed for the next ediôÙtion of PyôÙCon CZ, in Prague, Czech ReôÙpubôÙlic. AlôÙthough the schedôÙule is not yet ready, this talk will most likeôÙly be on June 8th.

The first two days of the conôÙferôÙence (June 8th and June 9th), will be for talkôÙs, whilst the last one (SatôÙurôÙday, June 10), will be for workôÙshops (sprints).

This is anôÙothôÙer inôÙterôÙestôÙing opôÙporôÙtuôÙniôÙty to share and learn from the Python ôÙcomôÙmuôÙniôÙty, as it was last year when I preôÙsentôÙed at EuôÙroPyôÙthon 2016. On this ocôÙcaôÙsion, I ôÙplan to post an enôÙtry with the events, per each day of the conôÙferôÙence.

item.title: A few ideas on code review
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
AfôÙter watchôÙing a talk about code reôÙview [1], from the last EuôÙroPyôÙthon, I got ôÙsome ideas that I'd like to sumôÙmarise.

Read moreãÎ (2 min remaining to read)

item.title: __wrapped__ in Python decorators
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This is anôÙothôÙer of the new inôÙterôÙestôÙing things that Python 3 has. Time ago, it was someôÙhow tricky to work with decôÙoôÙratôÙed funcôÙtionôÙs, beôÙcause the decôÙoôÙraôÙtor reôÙplaced the origôÙiôÙnal obôÙjecôÙt, and its beôÙhavôÙiour beôÙcame hard to reach.

Read moreãÎ (2 min remaining to read)

item.title: Running PostgreSQL in memory with docker
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Introduction

InôÙterôÙactôÙing with a database, can be a regôÙuôÙlar task of a deôÙvelôÙopôÙer, and for that we would like to enôÙsure that we are deôÙvelôÙopôÙing and testôÙing in sitôÙuôÙaôÙtions close ôÙto a reôÙal imôÙpleôÙmenôÙtaôÙtion; thereôÙfore, usôÙing the same dataôÙbase as inôÙ proôÙducôÙtion can help deôÙtectôÙing isôÙsues earôÙly.

HowôÙevôÙer, setôÙting up an enôÙtire dataôÙbase servôÙer for deôÙvelôÙopôÙmenôÙt, can be cumôÙberôÙsome. HopeôÙfulôÙly nowaôÙdays, modôÙern opôÙerôÙatôÙing sysôÙtems like LinôÙux have ôÙgreat tools and feaôÙtures that we could take adôÙvanôÙtage of.

In parôÙticôÙuôÙlar, I would like to setôÙup a simôÙple dataôÙbase loôÙcalôÙly usôÙing dockôÙer, and storôÙing the daôÙta in memôÙoôÙry.

The idea is simôÙple: run a dockôÙer conôÙtainôÙer with the imôÙage for PostôÙgresSQL, usôÙing a tmpfs [1] [2] as storôÙage for the dataôÙbase (a ramfs could alôÙso be used).

Read moreãÎ (3 min remaining to read)

item.title: 2016 in review
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
A reôÙview of the events that took place this year, reôÙlatôÙed to softôÙware engiôÙneerôÙing and arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture.

The conferences, talks, and events

These are some of the events I parôÙticôÙiôÙpatôÙed in:

  • (2 Apr 2016) PROôÙTOôÙSEC 2016 [1]: A techôÙniôÙcal conôÙferôÙence about netôÙworkôÙing, proôÙtoôÙcolôÙs, seôÙcuôÙriôÙty, and reôÙlatôÙed topôÙicôÙs.
  • (JuôÙly 2016) EuôÙroPyôÙthon 2016: I atôÙtendôÙed sevôÙerôÙal talkôÙs, trainôÙings, workôÙshopôÙs, and preôÙsentôÙed a talk.
  • (10 Sep 2016) Python Cû°rôÙdoôÙba meetôÙup [6]: inôÙterôÙestôÙing meetôÙup where I pôÙreôÙsentôÙed my talk at EuôÙroPyôÙthon and atôÙtendôÙed the sprints afôÙterôÙwardôÙs.
  • (14 Sep 2016) ArôÙqôÙConf MeetôÙup ô¢Cû°ôÙmo es traôÙbaôÙjar disôÙtribuiôÙdo globôÙalôÙmente? ôÙCaôÙso Open Stack [5]: HostôÙed in the Red Hat's ofôÙfices in Buenos Aires, this talk was a very inôÙterôÙestôÙing one, on which I learned about the colôÙlabôÙoôÙraôÙtive way of workôÙing of the Open Stack team, around the globe.
  • (17 Nov 2016) Python Barcelona meetôÙup.
  • (9 Dec 2016) Barcelona Spark meetôÙup [2]
  • (22 Dec 2016) Python Barcelona DeôÙcemôÙber meetôÙup [7]: I preôÙsentôÙed my talk about clean code in Python (yes, again :-), and there were two othôÙer inôÙterôÙestôÙing talkôÙs. Mine was the first one of the evening. The secôÙond talk, was about zc.buildôÙout, an inôÙterôÙestôÙing tool for manôÙagôÙing packôÙages and enôÙviôÙronôÙments in Python, which I first heard of at PyôÙconAr (Python ArôÙgentiôÙna group) 2012. The third and last talk, pôÙreôÙsentôÙed a project named aioôÙcache [8]: a cache sysôÙtem imôÙpleôÙmentôÙed in asynôÙcio, that supôÙports mulôÙtiôÙple backôÙ-endôÙs. The talk walked us through the ôÙcode, its arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, and some use casôÙes with demos.

Code wise

I startôÙed with a small conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtion to the coala project [3], and durôÙing the EuôÙroPyôÙthon sprints I conôÙtribôÙuted to aioôÙhttp with a pull reôÙquest.

There were two new reôÙleasôÙes of my vim conôÙfigôÙuôÙraôÙtion, verôÙsions v0.3 and v0.4 [4], which is great beôÙcause is the sinôÙgle tool I use evôÙery day, and it is nice to have sevôÙerôÙal imôÙproveôÙments on it.

I alôÙso enôÙhanced my dot files, but that is still in beôÙta.

On general

A miôÙnor reôÙlease of Python3.5, but most imôÙporôÙtantôÙly: Python3.6 was reôÙcentôÙly reôÙleased!

There were two reôÙleasôÙes of FeôÙdoôÙra, 24 and 25, and there were lots of imôÙproveôÙments and changes in the LinôÙux KerôÙnel.

ReôÙgardôÙing inôÙforôÙmaôÙtion seôÙcuôÙriôÙty, there were some isôÙsues in the LinôÙux KerôÙnel, alôÙso in apôÙpliôÙcaôÙtions (such as dirty COW, etc.), and a masôÙsive DDoS atôÙtack that stroke the InôÙterôÙnet on OcôÙtoôÙber, due to IoT deôÙvices beôÙing comôÙproôÙmised.

What to look forward to for the new year

  • I still look forôÙward for more conôÙtriôÙbuôÙtions to open source, in parôÙticôÙuôÙlar with CPython.
  • More funcôÙtionôÙal proôÙgramôÙming (SôÙcala/Haskell mayôÙbe), low-levôÙel proôÙgramôÙming inôÙ C, and reôÙsearch in the field of inôÙfosôÙec.
  • Keep on parôÙticôÙiôÙpatôÙing on techôÙniôÙcal conôÙferôÙences.

And a lot more.

[1]https://www.protosec.org/en/
[2]https://www.meetup.com/Spark-Barcelona/events/235763327/
[3]https://github.com/coala/coala
[4]https://github.com/rmariano/vim-config/releases
[5]https://www.meetup.com/es/ArquitecturaDeSoftware/events/233824352/
[6]https://www.meetup.com/es-ES/Cordoba-Python-Meetup/events/233741709/
[7]https://www.meetup.com/python-185/events/236196808/
[8]https://github.com/argaen/aiocache

item.title: Python Barcelona November meetup notes
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Last ThursôÙday NoôÙvemôÙber 17, 2016, there was a Python meetôÙup in Barcelona [1], with a set of inôÙterôÙestôÙing talkôÙs.

The first one was hostôÙed by two repôÙreôÙsenôÙtaôÙtives of the govôÙernôÙment of CatalunôÙya, and Barcelona city, and they preôÙsentôÙed the techôÙniôÙcal chalôÙlenges they are ôÙfacôÙing, and the new stack proôÙposed for onôÙcomôÙing projectôÙs. In this reôÙgard, it was inôÙterôÙestôÙing to see how they have lots of legaôÙcy apôÙpliôÙcaôÙtions writôÙten inôÙ J2EE, with JaôÙva frameôÙworkôÙs, that are outôÙdatôÙed, difôÙfiôÙcult to mainôÙtain, and they ôÙmenôÙtioned the idea of miôÙgratôÙing them to new, more modôÙern techôÙnoloôÙgies. In this sense, there are alôÙready projects in proôÙgress, and they chose Python + DjanôÙgo ôÙfor the miôÙgraôÙtion and re-imôÙpleôÙmenôÙtaôÙtion of the legaôÙcy sysôÙtemôÙs.

This was a very inôÙterôÙestôÙing in the sense that more than this was acôÙtuôÙalôÙly pôÙreôÙsentôÙed. In parôÙticôÙuôÙlar the idea of how the govôÙernôÙment wants to acôÙtuôÙalôÙly own their sysôÙtemôÙs, and thereôÙfore they are now choosôÙing open source softôÙware. It ôÙgoes beôÙyond than mereôÙly usôÙing Python and DjanôÙgo: they are alôÙso miôÙgratôÙing the ôÙworkôÙstaôÙtion maôÙchines to open source softôÙware (UbunôÙtu), and tryôÙing to earn more ôÙconôÙtracôÙtors that are starôÙtups rather than huge multiôÙnaôÙtionôÙals with proôÙpriôÙetary ôÙsoftôÙware. The idea is still the same: they want open data, transôÙparenôÙcy, and to acôÙtuôÙalôÙly own their sysôÙtemôÙs.

These sort of iniôÙtiaôÙtives are gainôÙing more and more tracôÙtion in the EuôÙroôÙpean UôÙnion, as many cities are startôÙing to shift toôÙwards an open govôÙernôÙmenôÙt, withôÙ open data, and more transôÙparenôÙcy. I look forôÙward to seeôÙing more projects inôÙ Python with open source techôÙnoloôÙgies, at their GitHub acôÙcount [2], in the ôÙforthôÙcomôÙing monthôÙs.

The secôÙond talk was about ConôÙda, and shortôÙly thereôÙafter a brief inôÙtroôÙducôÙtion, the rest of the preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtion was ôÙmostôÙly a deôÙmo (live codôÙing! :-), on which we saw sevôÙerôÙal exôÙamôÙples of inôÙstalling ôÙpackôÙages, exôÙportôÙing the enôÙviôÙronôÙmenôÙt, creôÙatôÙing a new one from a temôÙplate, etc. It was useôÙful to see both the strengths and shortôÙcomôÙings of the tool, what can and ôÙcanôÙnot be done with it, and how it can be useôÙful for deôÙvelôÙopôÙers that have to deal with sevôÙerôÙal sysôÙtem deôÙpenôÙdenôÙcies.

The third and last talk, was about PLONE, a CMS proôÙjecôÙt writôÙten in Python, and with its 15-year-old tenure, is the oldôÙest Python proôÙject (thing that I did not know). It was inôÙterôÙestôÙing to learn about itôÙs highôÙ-levôÙel arôÙchiôÙtecôÙture, comôÙpoôÙnents, and the way they work. Above that, I would ôÙlike to highôÙlight the reôÙcap that it was done about many unôÙderôÙapôÙpreôÙciôÙatôÙed zope liôÙbraries, that have been availôÙable in Python for a long time, doôÙing an amazôÙing job. It is now in my list, to reôÙview them.

All in alôÙl, it was a good meeôÙtup, and I liked very much the techôÙniôÙcal levôÙel of the talkôÙs, and the fact that the topôÙics were very diôÙverse, yet inôÙterôÙestôÙing. I would like to conôÙtinôÙue atôÙtendôÙing these meeôÙtupôÙs, and in the ôÙfuôÙture I might even subôÙmit a talk.

[1]https://www.meetup.com/es-ES/python-185/events/235058828/
[2]https://github.com/AjuntamentdeBarcelona
